premises.content: For three-dimensional normal-liquid He-3 in the conventional Landau Fermi-liquid framework used by Alvesalo et al. 1979, applying the standard mapping from gamma = 2.11 K^-1 to quasiparticle effective-mass renormalization gives m*/m approximately 2.12, and using the usual relation between m*/m and the first Landau parameter gives F1 approximately 3.36 [@Alvesalo1979].
premises.label: gcn_1587257a956f4d18

premises.content: For the normal-state liquid He-3 setting reported by Alvesalo et al. 1979, the observed molar specific heat per mole is linear for T >= about 3 mK, C/(nR) = gamma T with gamma = 2.11 +/- 0.02 K^-1; this measured linear region is assumed to represent the T -> 0 Fermi-liquid specific-heat coefficient used to infer m*/m and F1 [@Alvesalo1979].
premises.label: gcn_2ee995fe1e674e2a
